title: Add schema drift detection before DatabaseReady
summary: Review of fixes addressing external review #1 feedback from berendt. The diff refactors buildSchemaCheckJob to delegate to buildDBJob (W-001), adds a Chainsaw E2E test for schema drift detection (W-003), hardens the pymysql OperationalError handler with int() cast (W-005), and adds explicit cursor close before connection close (W-006). All changes are minimal, focused, and correct. Tests pass (33/33), go vet is clean, no new lint issues in changed files.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (33 unit tests, 8 builder + 25 reconciler)
Edge cases covered (stale Job recreation, db_sync failure bypasses schema-check, InstalledRelease not updated on failure)
E2E test added for happy-path schema drift detection
Existing tests updated to include completedSchemaCheckJob fixture

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (buildDBJob delegation matches buildDBSyncJob, buildUpgradeJob)
No code duplication (buildSchemaCheckJob eliminated ~30 lines of duplicated Job construction)
E2E test follows Chainsaw pattern (SPDX header, numbered steps, timeouts, catch blocks, unique CR name)
Review patterns documented for future reviews

security_checklist.item: Python script uses only SELECT (read-only, no DDL/DML)
OperationalError handler uses int() cast to prevent potential credential leakage
Sensitive data not exposed in error messages (only error code, not connection string)

architecture_checklist.item: buildSchemaCheckJob delegates to buildDBJob, eliminating duplication drift risk
Schema-check inherits future buildDBJob changes automatically (e.g., CC-0042 resource limits)
Solution is minimal — only overrides backoffLimit and TTL

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — shared Job construction via buildDBJob

fail_fast_checklist.item: Schema drift returns error (not just requeue) for controller logs and exponential backoff
Explicit cursor close in exception path prevents resource leaks

defensive_checklist.item: OperationalError.args[0] wrapped with int() for type safety
Cursor explicitly closed before connection in both happy and error paths

suggested_improvements: W-004 (inline condition reason strings) and W-007 (single-head migration assumption) from external review #1 were not addressed in this round. Both were marked as lower-priority by the external reviewer. Consider tracking them for a follow-up PR if the codebase moves toward typed constants for condition reasons.
